notes: Phase B self-test contract for SMS primitives. Twilio explicitly rejects send-to-self (error 21266 'To and From number cannot be the same'), so we can't do a true self-echo round-trip. Instead the receive_sms primitive is validated against externally-triggered inbound SMS (any service that texts our number, or a manual send from another phone). This contract documents the expected shape; full round-trip validation happens when a real phone-OTP-gated service signup flows through the verifier.

steps.description: Mint a unique nonce so waiting receive_sms can match a specific inbound message

Wait briefly for any externally-triggered inbound SMS carrying our nonce. Expected to time out in automated runs (no external trigger); passes when a real service's signup flow SMSes our number with the nonce embedded.
Basic reachability — expects 401 (no auth), proves api.twilio.com is reachable from the sandbox

steps.body: OnlyBots workflow probe — send primitive is wired up
steps.description: Exercise the send_sms primitive. Twilio will return HTTP 400 (21266) because trial accounts can't send to the same number — that's the expected outcome proving the primitive correctly reached Twilio and got a meaningful business response.
